hints.text: Use a while loop to check if the queue is not empty before dequeuing

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(std::deque<int>& dq) { if (dq.size() == 0) dq.push_back(0); return dq.front(); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean solve(java.util.Deque<Integer> dq) { if (dq.isEmpty()) dq.addLast(0); return dq.peek(); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, dq: list[int]) -> bool: if not dq: dq.append(0); return dq[0]
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(dq) { if (dq.length == 0) dq.push(0); return dq[0]; }
